executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 organizations compromised in the last 48 hours, including high-profile targets like Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health, and Kids & Company childcare. The Qilin ransomware group alone hit 21 Canadian entities. Additionally, the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security issued two critical alerts: a Linux vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431) and widespread social engineering attacks compromising enterprise cloud software environments. This represents a coordinated escalation requiring immediate defensive action across all sectors.

items.software_affected: Linux (specific distribution and version not specified in advisory)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a severe ransomware crisis with 103 confirmed organizational breaches reported on May 2, 2026, spanning all sectors and provinces. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security issued two critical advisories: AL26-009 addressing Linux vulnerability CVE-2026-31431 and AL26-010 warning of widespread social engineering attacks targeting enterprise cloud software (SaaS) environments. Public sector targets include municipalities, school boards, and health units; critical infrastructure includes telecommunications and energy companies.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (Consumer Services) compromised by Coinbase Cartel ransomware; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) compromised by Sinobi ransomware.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company breached by Everest ransomware with database leaked; Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi ransomware; Trucash (financial services) compromised by Lynx ransomware; multiple accounting firms including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, and reidhurstnagy.com also compromised.

report_2w.executive_summary: Microsoft is actively combating CVE-2026-32202, a zero-day Windows Shell vulnerability allowing credential theft without user interaction, reportedly exploited by Russian APT28. CISA added two actively exploited flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog: CVE-2024-1708 (ConnectWise ScreenConnect) and the Windows Shell flaw. VECT ransomware has been revealed as a wiper—victims paying ransoms cannot recover files over 131KB. A suspected Scattered Spider member was arrested in Finland, facing U.S. charges for cyberattacks. Vimeo disclosed a third-party breach via Anodot affecting user data, linked to ShinyHunters.

items.description: Windows Shell zero-day enabling NTLM hash theft via Defender SmartScreen bypass (zero-click)
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: Medium, Availability: Low

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.name: APT28 (Fancy Bear, Sofacy)
items.targets: Windows systems globally for credential theft
items.ttps: T1003.001 (LSASS Memory credential dumping), T1566 (Phishing), zero-click exploitation via CVE-2026-32202

items.name: Scattered Spider (UNC3944)
items.targets: Multiple organizations via social engineering and cyberattacks
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts), social engineering for initial access

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Vimeo, Pitney Bowes, Udemy, Aman Resorts via Anodot supply chain compromise
items.ttps: T1195.001 (Supply Chain Compromise - SaaS third-party), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage Object - Snowflake data warehouses)

items.name: Lazarus Group (BlueNoroff)
items.targets: Corporate systems, Web3 sector, macOS users via fake meeting invites
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1059.001 (PowerShell), ClickFix technique for fileless execution

items.name: GlassWorm
items.targets: Developers via malicious Open VSX code marketplace extensions
items.ttps: T1195.001 (Supply Chain Compromise - Software Supply Chain), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: BlackFile Extortion Group
items.targets: Retail and corporate sectors via vishing attacks
items.ttps: T1566.004 (Phishing: Spearphishing Voice - vishing), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

items.name: VECT Ransomware Operators (actually wiper)
items.targets: Windows, Linux, ESXi systems - files over 131KB irreversibly destroyed
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact - actually T1485 Data Destruction), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: APT-C-13 (Sandworm, FROZENBARENTS)
items.targets: Organizational infrastructure via SSH+TOR tunnels for covert persistence
items.ttps: T1090.003 (Proxy: Multi-hop Proxy - TOR), T1021.004 (SSH), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: BlobPhish Campaign
items.targets: Microsoft 365 users via memory-resident phishing using Blob URL APIs
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1056.003 (Input Capture: Web Portal Capture), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information)

items.name: MiniRAT
items.targets: macOS users via malicious npm packages
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise - Software Dependency), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: KYCShadow Android Banking Malware
items.targets: Android users via fake KYC workflows for credential and OTP theft
items.ttps: T1417 (Mobile: Input Capture), T1411 (Mobile: Input Injection), T1056 (Input Capture)

items.name: Iran-linked APT
items.targets: U.S. critical infrastructure
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1078 (Valid Accounts), persistent access to critical systems

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued two critical alerts: AL26-008 for cPanel/WHM vulnerability CVE-2026-41940, and updated AL25-012 for Cisco ASA/FTD vulnerabilities CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362. These advisories represent the official government guidance for Canadian organizations to patch immediately. No specific breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.
